free_text: Measurements of cross sections relevant to gamma-ray
line astronomy
The
techniques in extracting the angle integrated cross
sections.
Lawrence Berkeley Laboratory's 88-
Inch Cyclotron.
Two high purity Ge detectors of 109 cm volume.
The beams impinged on targets of C, N, 0, Mg,
Al, Si, and Fe, all of natural isotopic composition.
The target thicknesses varied from 200 microg/cm2 to
7.56 mg/cm2 for the various targets. Gamma rays
produced by various reactions were observed by Ge
detectors.
BP
The carbon target was a self-supporting 991
microg/cm2 thick foil of natural isotopic composition.
Absolute errors magnitude can be no more
accurate than the normalized data.
The systematic errors in data with several
checks and several repeated measurements. The estimate
of internal consistency of the data is 10%.
Table II, page 1812.

foil. The 1635 and 2315 lines produced in the decay
of the second excited state to the first excited
state and the decay of the first excited state to the
ground state of 14N.
